content:
MODBUS-RTU 
Field-Bus for electric actuator
Installation and Operation Manual

2 
Description 
MODBUS-RTU is a serial ﬁeldbus communication protocol to automate up to 247 electric actuators. 
This protocol is non-proprietary and cheap to build. This one of the most used industrial ﬁeldbuses because of its reliability. 
Bus type 
RS485 
Protocol  
MODBUS RTU, 16 bit CRC  
Baud  
9600 
Data bits 
8 
Parity  
none 
Stop bit  
1 
Distance  
1200 m 
Maximum number of slave devices per line/segment 
31 
Total number of slave devices 
247 with repeater 
Address range 
1-247 (0=broadcast)
Fonctions utilisables 
03 (0x03) Read Holding Registers: 
This function code is used to read the contents of a contiguous block of holding registers in a remote device. 
06 (0x06) Write Single Register: 
This function code is used to write a single holding register in a remote device. 
16 (0x10) Write Multiple registers: 
  This function code is used to write a block of contiguous registers  in a remote device. 
23 (0x17) Read/Write Multiple registers:

06 (0x06) Write Single Register: 
This function code is used to write a single holding register in a remote device. 
16 (0x10) Write Multiple registers: 
  This function code is used to write a block of contiguous registers  in a remote device. 
23 (0x17) Read/Write Multiple registers: 
 This function code performs a combination of one read operation and one write operation in a single MODBUS transaction. 
The write operation is performed before the read. 
The read possible registers are addresses from @0 to @41 (decimal)    - same as read 0x03 function 
The write possible registers are addresses from @20 to @29 (decimal) – same as write 0x10 function 
Broadcast 
broadcast is address 0, frame is understood but NO RESPONSE of the slave. No exception generated on address 0. 
Index 
Description  .................................................................................................................................  2

BBPR models (GS6) and 3-POSITION-BBPR models (GFS)  ..................................................................................................................    6 
POSI (GP8) and POSI-BBPR models (GPS)  ............................................................................................................................................    8 
VT+ and MT models  ...................................................................................................................  10 
RS485 transfers are immune to electromagnetic noise and unwanted signal. However, it’s mandatory to use
only twisted pair with earth connected shield and within the constraints imposed by EIA-485 norm.
The distance between MODBUS cables and others must be at least 20 cm.
CR-TEC Engineering Inc. • info@crtec.com • www.crtec.com • 203-318-9500

3 
MODBUS-RTU network structure  
A RS485 line allows the association of a PLC (master node) to 31 devices (slave nodes) over a maximum of 1200 meters 
MODBUS-RTU protocol provides addressing capacity for up to 247 Inputs, also, it’s possible to use repeaters to connect 
several lines and automate up to 247 actuators over a greater distance.  
Single line network (one RS485 line)

MODBUS

JP1=ON 
Up to 31 actuators 
Shielded twisted pairs according to EIA-485

Standard models (G00) and 3-position models  
SNAA720000 & SNAA720100 
SNAA730000 & SNAA730100 
A-0-B 
Electric connection 
Connect the MODBUS communication wire on RS485 terminal (0-A-B). 
Connect the power supply on the connector Ca 
1 : neutral (50/60 Hz) or negative (DC)
4 : phase (50/60 Hz) or positive (DC)

RS485 terminal (serial link) for MODBUS communication 
Use the supplied plug-in male connector Phoenix MC 1,5/3-ST-3,5 (3.5 mm, 3 ways, 8 A 
with screw tightening for wires from 16 to 28 AWG and 1.5 mm2 section). 
JP1 
Termination resistor jumper (Rt=120 Ω) for line last actuator

BBPR (GS6) and 3-POSITION-BBPR (GFS) models 
SNBA130000 & SNBA140000 
Electric connection 
Connect the MODBUS communication wire on the RS485 terminal of the BBPR board (0-A-B) 
Connect the power supply on the terminal Ca of the main board (see page 16). 
1 : neutral (50/60 Hz) or negative (DC)
3 : phase (50/60 Hz) or positive (DC)
For further information, please refer to the actuator manuals 
RS485 terminal (serial link) for 
MODBUS-RTU communication 
To be controled, the 
actuator must be in 
manual mode 
Terminating resistor activation 
jumper (ST1). 
If there’s no jumper on the board 
(previous version), it is necessary 
to connect a terminating resistor of 
120 Ω between terminals A and B.

POSI (GP8) and POSI-BBPR (GPS) models 
SNBA150000 
Electric connection 
Connect the MODBUS communication wire on the RS485 terminal of the GPS board (0-A-B) 
Connect the power supply on the terminal Ca of the main board (see p. 16). 
1 : neutral (50/60 Hz) or negative (DC)
3 : phase (50/60 Hz) or positive (DC)
For further information, please refer to the actuator manuals 
RS485 terminal (serial link) for MODBUS-RTU 
communication 
To be controled, the actuator must be in 
manual mode 
Terminating resistor activation jumper (ST1). 
If there’s no jumper on the board (previous version), 
it is necessary to connect a terminating resistor of 
120 Ω between terminals A and B.
